# godon

> Open Source Optimization Engine for AI Pilots and Humans

godon is a parallelized metaheuristics-driven live system optimization engine.
It optimizes real infrastructure and platform configurations using empirical validation
through meta-heuristic algorithms (TPE, NSGA-II, NSGA-III, QMC).

godon acts as the empirical validation layer between AI/LLM-generated configuration
suggestions and real-world system verification.

## Key Features

- Empirical validation of AI-suggested configurations against live systems
- Infrastructure-first, network-extensible (SSH, HTTP, Kubernetes API)
- Training-free, GPU-optional — no models to train
- Built-in guardrails, rollback, and safety mechanisms
- Multi-objective optimization with cooperative parallel workers
- Kubernetes-native deployment via Helm chart

## Documentation

- [Purpose](purpose/) — Problem statement, AI-era positioning, capabilities and caveats
- [Setup](setup/) — Helm chart deployment guide (local kind + existing cluster)
- [Architecture](architecture/) — System design, components, optimization loop, scaling
- [Breeder](concept_breeder/) — Optimization driver: pluggable algorithms, worker collaboration, guardrails, rollback, cooperation
- [Effectuator](concept_effectuator/) — Configuration application: SSH, HTTP, Kubernetes channels, idempotency
- [Reconnaissance](concept_reconnaissance/) — Observation layer: Prometheus, HTTP, script sources, timing, aggregation
- [Guardrails](concept_guardrails/) — Safety limits: soft/hard guardrails, rollback strategies, multi-objective interaction
- [Configuration Guide](config_guide/) — YAML configuration: search space, objectives, effectuator, recon, guardrails
- [Comparison](comparison/) — vs Optuna, Hyperopt, Nevergrad, Ray Tune, Ax, Akamas, StormForge, Datadog, KEDA
- [API Reference](api/) — OpenAPI spec for breeder management endpoints
